authors: Thomson SJ, Tavakkolizadeh M, Love-Jones S, Patel NK, Gu JW, Bains A, Doan Q, Moffitt M
title: Effects of Rate on Analgesia in Kilohertz Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation: Results of the PROCO Randomized Controlled Trial
journal_name: Neuromodulation: Technology at the Neural Interface
study_question: Do stimulation rates from 1-10 kHz provide equivalent pain relief in spinal cord stimulation?
population_assessed: 20 patients with chronic low back pain with or without leg pain, implanted with SCS systems
outcome_measures: Electronic diary numeric rating scale pain scores, charge per second, quality of life measures
inclusion_criteria: Persistent low back pain ≥90 days, ≥90 days pain management, mean pain ≥5, stable opioids, ODI 20-80
answer_to_study_question: All frequencies 1-10 kHz provided equivalent pain relief. 1 kHz required 60-70% less charge than higher frequencies.
